Implementation of the ECMA Date.prototype object, including methods for date and time manipulation.

# Purpose
The code defines the implementation of the `Date.prototype` object in the ECMAScript language, specifically for a JavaScript engine. It provides a set of functions that correspond to the methods available on the `Date.prototype` object as specified in the ECMAScript 5.1 standard (ECMA-262 v5). These functions include methods for converting date objects to strings (`toString`, `toDateString`, `toTimeString`, etc.), getting and setting various components of a date (such as year, month, day, hours, minutes, seconds, and milliseconds), and handling both local and UTC time representations.

The code is organized into several static functions, each implementing a specific method of the `Date.prototype` object. It uses macros and helper functions to manage common operations, such as converting values to numbers and handling errors. The code also includes conditional compilation to exclude certain features if the `CONFIG_DISABLE_DATE_BUILTIN` flag is set. This file is part of a larger codebase that implements the ECMAScript standard, and it interacts with other components through function calls and macros defined in included headers. The functions return `ecma_value_t` types, which must be managed using specific memory management functions (`ecma_free_value`).
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `math.h`
- `ecma-alloc.h`
- `ecma-builtin-helpers.h`
- `ecma-exceptions.h`
- `ecma-function-object.h`
- `ecma-globals.h`
- `ecma-helpers.h`
- `ecma-objects.h`
- `ecma-try-catch-macro.h`
- `ecma-builtins-internal.h`
- `ecma-builtin-internal-routines-template.inc.h`

### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_to\_string<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_to_string}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L56>)

Converts a date object to a string representation, handling invalid dates by returning 'Invalid Date'.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, expected to be a date object.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` with an empty simple value.
    - Use `ECMA_TRY_CATCH` to attempt to get the primitive value of `this_arg` as `prim_value`.
    - Convert `prim_value` to a number `prim_num`.
    - Check if `prim_num` is NaN; if true, set `ret_value` to the string 'Invalid Date'.
    - If `prim_num` is not NaN, convert `prim_num` to a string representation of the date and set it to `ret_value`.
    - Finalize the `prim_value` using `ECMA_FINALIZE`.
    - Return `ret_value`.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the string form of the date or 'Invalid Date' if the date is not valid.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_date_get_primitive_value`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_get_primitive_value>)
    - [`ecma_get_magic_string`](<../base/ecma-helpers-string.c.md#ecma_get_magic_string>)
    - [`ecma_date_value_to_string`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_value_to_string>)

### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_to\_date\_string<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_to_date_string}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L91>)

Converts a Date object to a string representing the date portion.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, expected to be a Date object.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` with an empty simple value.
    - Check if `this_arg` is an object and if its class name is 'Date'.
    - If not, raise a type error and set `ret_value` to the error value.
    - If it is a Date object, convert `this_arg` to an object and store it in `obj_this`.
    - Retrieve the internal date property from `obj_this`.
    - Check if the date number is NaN.
    - If it is NaN, set `ret_value` to the string 'Invalid Date'.
    - If it is not NaN, convert the date number to a date string and set `ret_value` to this string.
    - Finalize the `obj_this` value.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the date string or an error message if the input is not a Date object.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_object_get_class_name`](<../operations/ecma-objects.c.md#ecma_object_get_class_name>)
    - [`ecma_get_object_from_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_get_object_from_value>)
    - [`ecma_raise_type_error`](<../operations/ecma-exceptions.c.md#ecma_raise_type_error>)
    - [`ecma_op_to_object`](<../operations/ecma-conversion.c.md#ecma_op_to_object>)
    - [`ecma_get_magic_string`](<../base/ecma-helpers-string.c.md#ecma_get_magic_string>)
    - [`ecma_date_value_to_date_string`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_value_to_date_string>)

### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_to\_time\_string<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_to_time_string}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L137>)

Converts a Date object to a time string representation.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, expected to be a Date object.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` with an empty simple value.
    - Check if `this_arg` is an object and if its class name is 'Date'.
    - If not, raise a type error and set `ret_value` to the error value.
    - If it is a Date object, attempt to convert `this_arg` to an object and store it in `obj_this`.
    - Retrieve the internal property `ECMA_INTERNAL_PROPERTY_DATE_FLOAT` from the object and get its numeric value.
    - Check if the numeric value is NaN; if so, set `ret_value` to the string 'Invalid Date'.
    - If the numeric value is not NaN, convert it to a time string using [`ecma_date_value_to_time_string`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_value_to_time_string>) and set `ret_value`.
    - Finalize the `obj_this` value.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the time string of the Date object or an error message if the input is not a Date object.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_object_get_class_name`](<../operations/ecma-objects.c.md#ecma_object_get_class_name>)
    - [`ecma_get_object_from_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_get_object_from_value>)
    - [`ecma_raise_type_error`](<../operations/ecma-exceptions.c.md#ecma_raise_type_error>)
    - [`ecma_op_to_object`](<../operations/ecma-conversion.c.md#ecma_op_to_object>)
    - [`ecma_get_magic_string`](<../base/ecma-helpers-string.c.md#ecma_get_magic_string>)
    - [`ecma_date_value_to_time_string`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_value_to_time_string>)

### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_get\_time<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L243>)

Retrieves the time value from a Date object or raises a type error if the input is not a Date object.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, expected to be a Date object.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `this_arg` is an object using `ecma_is_value_object`.
    - Retrieve the object pointer `obj_p` from `this_arg` using [`ecma_get_object_from_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_get_object_from_value>).
    - Check if the class name of `obj_p` is `LIT_MAGIC_STRING_DATE_UL`.
    - Retrieve the internal property `ECMA_INTERNAL_PROPERTY_DATE_FLOAT` from `obj_p`.
    - Get the internal number value `date_num_p` from the property.
    - Return the number value as an `ecma_value_t` using [`ecma_make_number_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_number_value>).
    - If any check fails, return a type error using [`ecma_raise_type_error`](<../operations/ecma-exceptions.c.md#ecma_raise_type_error>).
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the time value of the Date object, or a type error if the input is not a Date object.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_get_object_from_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_get_object_from_value>)
    - [`ecma_object_get_class_name`](<../operations/ecma-objects.c.md#ecma_object_get_class_name>)
    - [`ecma_make_number_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_number_value>)
    - [`ecma_raise_type_error`](<../operations/ecma-exceptions.c.md#ecma_raise_type_error>)

### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_set\_time<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_set_time}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L337>)

Sets the time value of a Date object to a specified time and returns the new time value.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, expected to be a Date object.
    - ``time``: The time value to set, which will be converted to a number.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` with an empty simple value.
    - Check if `this_arg` is an object and if its class name is 'Date'.
    - If not, raise a type error and set `ret_value` to the error value.
    - If `this_arg` is a Date object, convert `time` to a number using `ECMA_OP_TO_NUMBER_TRY_CATCH`.
    - Clip the number to a valid date time value using `ecma_date_time_clip`.
    - Retrieve the internal date property from the Date object and update it with the clipped time value.
    - Set `ret_value` to the new time value as a number.
    - Finalize the number conversion operation.
- **Output**: The new time value of the Date object as an `ecma_value_t` number.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_object_get_class_name`](<../operations/ecma-objects.c.md#ecma_object_get_class_name>)
    - [`ecma_get_object_from_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_get_object_from_value>)
    - [`ecma_raise_type_error`](<../operations/ecma-exceptions.c.md#ecma_raise_type_error>)
    - [`ecma_make_number_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_number_value>)

### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_set\_milliseconds<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_set_milliseconds}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L380>)

Sets the milliseconds of a date object to a specified value and updates the internal time representation accordingly.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, which should be a date object.
    - ``ms``: The millisecond value to set.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` with an empty simple value.
    - Attempt to get the current time value of `this_arg` using [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>) and convert it to local time.
    - Convert `ms` to a number and store it in `milli`.
    - Extract the hour, minute, and second from the local time `t`.
    - Set the internal date property of `this_arg` using the day from `t`, the time made from hour, minute, second, and `milli`, and the local time zone.
    - Finalize the conversion of `milli` and the retrieval of `this_time_value`.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the updated date object, which must be freed with `ecma_free_value`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_set_internal_property`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_set_internal_property>)
    - [`ecma_date_make_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_make_time>)

### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_set\_utc\_milliseconds<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_set_utc_milliseconds}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L417>)

Sets the UTC milliseconds of a date object.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, which should be a date object.
    - ``ms``: The milliseconds value to set.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` with an empty simple value.
    - Try to get the current time value of `this_arg` using [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>) and store it in `this_time_value`.
    - Convert `this_time_value` to a number `t`.
    - Convert `ms` to a number `milli`.
    - Extract the hour, minute, and second from `t`.
    - Set the internal date property of `this_arg` using the day from `t`, the time created from hour, minute, second, and `milli`, and the UTC flag.
    - Finalize the conversion of `milli` and `this_time_value`.
- **Output**: Returns an `ecma_value_t` representing the updated date object with the new UTC milliseconds.
- **Functions Called**:

### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_set\_seconds<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_set_seconds}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L454>)

Sets the seconds and optionally milliseconds of a date object, adjusting the time accordingly.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, expected to be a date object.
    - ``sec``: The seconds value to set.
    - ``ms``: The optional milliseconds value to set.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` to an empty simple value.
    - Try to get the current time value of `this_arg` using [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>) and convert it to local time.
    - Convert `sec` to a number and store it in `s`.
    - Convert `ms` to a number and store it in `milli`; if `ms` is undefined, set `milli` to the current milliseconds from `t`.
    - Extract the hour and minute from `t`.
    - Set the internal date property of `this_arg` using the day from `t`, the new time composed of `hour`, `min`, `s`, and `milli`, and the local time zone.
    - Finalize the number conversions and return `ret_value`.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the updated date object, which must be freed with `ecma_free_value`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_set_internal_property`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_set_internal_property>)
    - [`ecma_date_make_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_make_time>)


---
### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_set\_utc\_seconds<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_set_utc_seconds}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L499>)

Sets the seconds and optional milliseconds of a date object in UTC time.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, expected to be a date object.
    - ``sec``: The seconds to set, as an `ecma_value_t`.
    - ``ms``: The optional milliseconds to set, as an `ecma_value_t`; if undefined, the current milliseconds are used.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` to an empty simple value.
    - Try to get the current time value from `this_arg` using [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>) and convert it to a number `t`.
    - Convert `sec` to a number `s`, handling any errors.
    - Convert `ms` to a number `milli`, or use the current milliseconds from `t` if `ms` is undefined.
    - Extract the hour and minute from `t`.
    - Set the internal date property of `this_arg` using the day from `t`, the constructed time from `hour`, `min`, `s`, and `milli`, and the UTC flag.
    - Finalize the number conversions and return `ret_value`.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the result of setting the seconds and milliseconds, which must be freed with `ecma_free_value`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_set_internal_property`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_set_internal_property>)
    - [`ecma_date_make_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_make_time>)

### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_set\_minutes<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_set_minutes}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L544>)

Sets the minutes, seconds, and milliseconds of a date object based on provided arguments.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, representing the date object to modify.
    - ``args``: An array of arguments where the first element is the minutes, the second is the seconds, and the third is the milliseconds.
    - ``args_number``: The number of arguments provided in `args`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` to an empty simple value.
    - Try to get the current time value of `this_arg` and convert it to local time `t`.
    - Initialize `m` to NaN, `s` to the seconds from `t`, and `milli` to the milliseconds from `t`.
    - If at least one argument is provided and it is not undefined, convert the first argument to a number and assign it to `m`.
    - If a second argument is provided and it is not undefined, convert it to a number and assign it to `s`.
    - If a third argument is provided and it is not undefined, convert it to a number and assign it to `milli`.
    - If `ret_value` is still empty, calculate the hour from `t` and set the internal date property of `this_arg` using the day from `t`, the calculated time, and the local time zone.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the updated date object, which must be freed with `ecma_free_value`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>)
    - [`ecma_number_make_nan`](<../base/ecma-helpers-number.c.md#ecma_number_make_nan>)
    - [`ecma_date_set_internal_property`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_set_internal_property>)
    - [`ecma_date_make_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_make_time>)

    - ``args``: An array of arguments where the first element is the minutes, the second is the seconds, and the third is the milliseconds.
    - ``args_number``: The number of arguments provided in `args`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` to an empty simple value.
    - Try to get the current time value of `this_arg` using [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>).
    - Extract the current time components: seconds and milliseconds from the time value `t`.
    - If at least one argument is provided and it is not undefined, convert the first argument to a number and assign it to `m` (minutes).
    - If a second argument is provided and it is not undefined, convert it to a number and assign it to `s` (seconds).
    - If a third argument is provided and it is not undefined, convert it to a number and assign it to `milli` (milliseconds).
    - If `ret_value` is still empty, calculate the hour from the time value `t` and set the internal date property using the calculated day, time, and UTC flag.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the updated date object, which must be freed with `ecma_free_value`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>)
    - [`ecma_number_make_nan`](<../base/ecma-helpers-number.c.md#ecma_number_make_nan>)
    - [`ecma_date_set_internal_property`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_set_internal_property>)
    - [`ecma_date_make_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_make_time>)

### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_set\_hours<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_set_hours}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L666>)

Sets the hours, minutes, seconds, and milliseconds of a date object based on provided arguments.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, which should be a date object.
    - ``args``: An array of arguments where the first element is the hour, the second is the minute, the third is the second, and the fourth is the millisecond.
    - ``args_number``: The number of arguments provided in `args`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` to an empty simple value.
    - Try to get the current time value of `this_arg` and convert it to local time `t`.
    - Initialize `h`, `m`, `s`, and `milli` to NaN or current time components from `t`.
    - If `args_number` is greater than 0 and the first argument is not undefined, convert it to a number and assign it to `h`.
    - If `args_number` is greater than 1 and the second argument is not undefined, convert it to a number and assign it to `m`.
    - If `args_number` is greater than 2 and the third argument is not undefined, convert it to a number and assign it to `s`.
    - If `args_number` is greater than 3 and the fourth argument is not undefined, convert it to a number and assign it to `milli`.
    - If `ret_value` is still empty, set the internal date property of `this_arg` using the calculated day and time components.
    - Finalize the try-catch block for `this_time_value`.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the result of setting the date's time components, which must be freed with `ecma_free_value`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>)
    - [`ecma_number_make_nan`](<../base/ecma-helpers-number.c.md#ecma_number_make_nan>)
    - [`ecma_date_set_internal_property`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_set_internal_property>)
    - [`ecma_date_make_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_make_time>)

### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_set\_utc\_hours<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_set_utc_hours}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L735>)

Sets the UTC hours, minutes, seconds, and milliseconds of a date object based on provided arguments.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, which should be a date object.
    - ``args``: An array of arguments representing the hours, minutes, seconds, and milliseconds to set.
    - ``args_number``: The number of arguments provided in `args`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` to an empty simple value.
    - Retrieve the current time value of the date object using [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>).
    - Initialize `h`, `m`, `s`, and `milli` to NaN or current time components from `t`.
    - If `args_number` is greater than 0 and `args[0]` is not undefined, convert `args[0]` to a number and assign it to `h`.
    - If `args_number` is greater than 1 and `args[1]` is not undefined, convert `args[1]` to a number and assign it to `m`.
    - If `args_number` is greater than 2 and `args[2]` is not undefined, convert `args[2]` to a number and assign it to `s`.
    - If `args_number` is greater than 3 and `args[3]` is not undefined, convert `args[3]` to a number and assign it to `milli`.
    - If `ret_value` is still empty, set the internal date property using [`ecma_date_set_internal_property`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_set_internal_property>) with the new time components in UTC.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the result of setting the UTC hours, which must be freed with `ecma_free_value`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>)

### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_set\_date<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_set_date}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L804>)

Sets the day of the month for a date object using local time.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, expected to be a date object.
    - ``date``: The day of the month to set, as an `ecma_value_t`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` to an empty simple value.
    - Use `ECMA_TRY_CATCH` to get the current time value of `this_arg` and convert it to local time, storing it in `t`.
    - Convert `date` to a number using `ECMA_OP_TO_NUMBER_TRY_CATCH`, storing it in `dt`.
    - Extract the year and month from `t` using [`ecma_date_year_from_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_year_from_time>) and [`ecma_date_month_from_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_month_from_time>).
    - Set the internal date property of `this_arg` using [`ecma_date_set_internal_property`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_set_internal_property>), with the new day calculated by [`ecma_date_make_day`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_make_day>) and the time within the day from `ecma_date_time_within_day`.
    - Finalize the number conversion and the try-catch block.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the result of setting the date, which must be freed with `ecma_free_value`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_year_from_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_year_from_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_month_from_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_month_from_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_set_internal_property`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_set_internal_property>)
    - [`ecma_date_make_day`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_make_day>)

### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_set\_utc\_date<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_set_utc_date}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L840>)

Sets the UTC date of a Date object using the provided date value.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, expected to be a Date object.
    - ``date``: The new date value to set, which will be converted to a number.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` with an empty simple value.
    - Try to get the current time value of the Date object using [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>) and store it in `this_time_value`.
    - Convert `this_time_value` to a number `t`.
    - Convert the `date` argument to a number `dt`.
    - Extract the year and month from the time `t`.
    - Set the internal date property of the Date object using [`ecma_date_set_internal_property`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_set_internal_property>) with the new day calculated from `year`, `month`, and `dt`, and the time within the day from `t`, in UTC.
    - Finalize the number conversion of `dt` and the try-catch block for `this_time_value`.
- **Output**: Returns an `ecma_value_t` representing the result of setting the date, which must be freed with `ecma_free_value`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_year_from_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_year_from_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_month_from_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_month_from_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_set_internal_property`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_set_internal_property>)
    - [`ecma_date_make_day`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_make_day>)

### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_set\_month<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_set_month}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L876>)

Sets the month and optionally the date of a Date object, adjusting the time value accordingly.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The Date object whose month is to be set.
    - ``month``: The month value to set, where January is 0 and December is 11.
    - ``date``: The optional date value to set; if not provided, the current date of the month is used.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` to an empty simple value.
    - Try to get the current time value of `this_arg` using [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>) and convert it to local time.
    - Convert `month` to a number and store it in `m`.
    - Convert `date` to a number and store it in `dt`; if `date` is undefined, set `dt` to the current date from `t`.
    - Calculate the year from `t` and set the internal date property of `this_arg` using the calculated day and time within the day.
    - Finalize the number conversions and return `ret_value`.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the updated time value of the Date object, which must be freed with `ecma_free_value`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_date_from_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_date_from_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_year_from_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_year_from_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_set_internal_property`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_set_internal_property>)
    - [`ecma_date_make_day`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_make_day>)


---
### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_set\_utc\_month<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_set_utc_month}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L920>)

Sets the month and optionally the date of a Date object in UTC time.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The Date object to modify.
    - ``month``: The month to set, where 0 represents January.
    - ``date``: The date to set; if undefined, the current date of the month is used.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` to an empty simple value.
    - Try to get the current time value of `this_arg` using [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>).
    - Convert `month` to a number and store it in `m`.
    - Convert `date` to a number and store it in `dt`; if `date` is undefined, set `dt` to the current date from `t`.
    - Calculate the year from `t` and set the internal date property of `this_arg` using [`ecma_date_set_internal_property`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_set_internal_property>) with the new day and time within the day in UTC.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the updated Date object or an error if the operation fails.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_date_from_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_date_from_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_year_from_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_year_from_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_set_internal_property`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_set_internal_property>)
    - [`ecma_date_make_day`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_make_day>)

### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_set\_full\_year<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_set_full_year}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L964>)

Sets the full year, and optionally the month and date, of a Date object using local time.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, expected to be a Date object.
    - ``args``: An array of arguments where the first element is the year, the second is the month (optional), and the third is the date (optional).
    - ``args_number``: The number of arguments provided in `args`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` to an empty simple value.
    - Try to get the current time value of the Date object using [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>) and convert it to local time.
    - If the time value is NaN, set it to zero.
    - Initialize `y`, `m`, and `dt` to NaN, the current month, and the current date from the time value, respectively.
    - If at least one argument is provided and is not undefined, convert the first argument to a number and assign it to `y`.
    - If a second argument is provided and is not undefined, convert it to a number and assign it to `m`.
    - If a third argument is provided and is not undefined, convert it to a number and assign it to `dt`.
    - If `ret_value` is still empty, set the internal date property using [`ecma_date_set_internal_property`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_set_internal_property>) with the calculated day and time within the day.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the result of setting the full year, month, and date, which must be freed with `ecma_free_value`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>)
    - [`ecma_number_make_nan`](<../base/ecma-helpers-number.c.md#ecma_number_make_nan>)
    - [`ecma_date_month_from_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_month_from_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_date_from_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_date_from_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_set_internal_property`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_set_internal_property>)
    - [`ecma_date_make_day`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_make_day>)

### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_set\_utc\_full\_year<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_set_utc_full_year}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L1028>)

Sets the full year, month, and date of a Date object in UTC time based on provided arguments.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, which should be a Date object.
    - ``args``: An array of arguments where the first element is the year, the second is the month, and the third is the date.
    - ``args_number``: The number of arguments provided in `args`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` to an empty simple value.
    - Try to get the current time value of `this_arg` using [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>).
    - If the time value is NaN, set `t` to zero.
    - Initialize `y`, `m`, and `dt` to NaN, the current month, and the current date from `t`, respectively.
    - If at least one argument is provided and is not undefined, convert the first argument to a number and assign it to `y`.
    - If a second argument is provided and is not undefined, convert it to a number and assign it to `m`.
    - If a third argument is provided and is not undefined, convert it to a number and assign it to `dt`.
    - If `ret_value` is still empty, set the internal date property of `this_arg` using [`ecma_date_set_internal_property`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_set_internal_property>) with the calculated day and time within the day in UTC.
- **Output**: Returns an `ecma_value_t` which is the result of setting the internal date property, or an error if any occurred during processing.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>)
    - [`ecma_number_make_nan`](<../base/ecma-helpers-number.c.md#ecma_number_make_nan>)
    - [`ecma_date_month_from_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_month_from_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_date_from_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_date_from_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_set_internal_property`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_set_internal_property>)
    - [`ecma_date_make_day`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_make_day>)

### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_to\_utc\_string<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_to_utc_string}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L1092>)

Converts a date object to a UTC string representation.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, expected to be a date object.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` with an empty simple value.
    - Use `ECMA_TRY_CATCH` to attempt to get the primitive value from `this_arg`.
    - Convert the primitive value to a number `prim_num`.
    - Check if `prim_num` is NaN; if true, set `ret_value` to the string 'Invalid Date'.
    - If `prim_num` is not NaN, convert it to a UTC string using [`ecma_date_value_to_utc_string`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_value_to_utc_string>).
    - Finalize the `prim_value` using `ECMA_FINALIZE`.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the UTC string of the date or 'Invalid Date' if the date is not valid.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_date_get_primitive_value`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_get_primitive_value>)
    - [`ecma_get_magic_string`](<../base/ecma-helpers-string.c.md#ecma_get_magic_string>)
    - [`ecma_date_value_to_utc_string`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_value_to_utc_string>)


---
### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_to\_iso\_string<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_to_iso_string}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L1127>)

Converts a date object to an ISO 8601 string representation.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, expected to be a date object.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` with an empty simple value.
    - Use `ECMA_TRY_CATCH` to attempt to get the primitive value of `this_arg` as `prim_value`.
    - Convert `prim_value` to a number `prim_num`.
    - Check if `prim_num` is NaN or infinity; if true, set `ret_value` to a range error.
    - If `prim_num` is a valid number, convert it to an ISO string using [`ecma_date_value_to_iso_string`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_value_to_iso_string>).
    - Finalize the `prim_value` using `ECMA_FINALIZE`.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the ISO 8601 string of the date, or a range error if the date is invalid.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_date_get_primitive_value`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_get_primitive_value>)
    - [`ecma_number_is_infinity`](<../base/ecma-helpers-number.c.md#ecma_number_is_infinity>)
    - [`ecma_raise_range_error`](<../operations/ecma-exceptions.c.md#ecma_raise_range_error>)
    - [`ecma_date_value_to_iso_string`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_value_to_iso_string>)


---
### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_to\_json<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_to_json}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L1161>)

Converts a Date object to a JSON-compatible string using the `toISOString` method if applicable.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, expected to be a Date object.
    - ``arg``: A key argument, which is unused in this function.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Convert `this_arg` to an object using [`ecma_op_to_object`](<../operations/ecma-conversion.c.md#ecma_op_to_object>) and store the result in `obj`.
    - Convert `obj` to a primitive value with a preferred type of number using [`ecma_op_to_primitive`](<../operations/ecma-conversion.c.md#ecma_op_to_primitive>), storing the result in `tv`.
    - Check if `tv` is a number; if it is NaN or Infinity, set `ret_value` to null.
    - If `ret_value` is still empty, retrieve the `toISOString` method from the object and store it in `to_iso`.
    - Check if `to_iso` is callable; if not, raise a type error.
    - If `to_iso` is callable, call it with `this_arg` and store the result in `ret_value`.
    - Finalize and clean up all temporary values and references.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the JSON string or null if the date is invalid.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_op_to_object`](<../operations/ecma-conversion.c.md#ecma_op_to_object>)
    - [`ecma_op_to_primitive`](<../operations/ecma-conversion.c.md#ecma_op_to_primitive>)
    - [`ecma_number_is_infinity`](<../base/ecma-helpers-number.c.md#ecma_number_is_infinity>)

### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_get\_year<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_year}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L1233>)

Calculates the year from a date object, subtracting 1900, or returns 'NaN' if the date is invalid.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The `this` argument, expected to be a date object.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` with an empty simple value.
    - Use `ECMA_TRY_CATCH` to attempt to get the time value from `this_arg` using [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>).
    - Convert the obtained value to a number `this_num`.
    - Check if `this_num` is NaN using `ecma_number_is_nan`.
    - If `this_num` is NaN, set `ret_value` to the string 'NaN'.
    - If `this_num` is not NaN, calculate the year from the local time of `this_num`, subtract 1900, and set `ret_value` to this number.
    - Finalize the `ECMA_TRY_CATCH` block, ensuring resources are freed.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the year minus 1900, or the string 'NaN' if the date is invalid.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>)
    - [`ecma_get_magic_string`](<../base/ecma-helpers-string.c.md#ecma_get_magic_string>)
    - [`ecma_date_year_from_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_year_from_time>)
    - [`ecma_make_number_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_number_value>)


---
### ecma\_builtin\_date\_prototype\_set\_year<!-- {{#callable:ecma_builtin_date_prototype_set_year}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/builtin-objects/ecma-builtin-date-prototype.c#L1267>)

Sets the year of a date object, adjusting for two-digit years by adding 1900, and updates the internal date properties accordingly.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``this_arg``: The date object whose year is to be set.
    - ``year``: The year value to set, which can be a two-digit or full year.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `ret_value` to an empty simple value.
    - Try to get the time value of `this_arg` using [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>).
    - Convert the time to local time and check if it is NaN; if so, set it to zero.
    - Convert `year` to a number and store it in `y`.
    - If `y` is NaN, set the internal date property of `this_arg` to zero with NaN year in UTC.
    - If `y` is between 0 and 99, add 1900 to `y`.
    - If `ret_value` is still empty, calculate the month and date from the time, and set the internal date property of `this_arg` with the new year, month, and date in UTC.
- **Output**: An `ecma_value_t` representing the updated date object, which must be freed with `ecma_free_value`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ecma_make_simple_value`](<../base/ecma-helpers-value.c.md#ecma_make_simple_value>)
    - [`ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time`](<#ecma_builtin_date_prototype_get_time>)
    - [`ecma_number_make_nan`](<../base/ecma-helpers-number.c.md#ecma_number_make_nan>)
    - [`ecma_date_set_internal_property`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_set_internal_property>)
    - [`ecma_date_month_from_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_month_from_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_date_from_time`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_date_from_time>)
    - [`ecma_date_make_day`](<ecma-builtin-helpers-date.c.md#ecma_date_make_day>)
